text: Sleep extension interventions ranging from 26-106 additional minutes of time in bed or total sleep time may improve sports performance in athletes.
Of fifteen sports performance measures assessed after sleep extension, six showed large effect sizes, while others ranged from trivial to medium.
specific_value: 6 of 15 measures showed large effect size

text: The magnitude of performance benefit from sleep extension is dependent on the specific performance variable assessed, with some measures responding much more than others.

The current evidence base for sleep extension benefiting athletic performance is very limited, with only 5 of 74 screened studies meeting eligibility criteria and evidence quality ranging from very low to moderate.
specific_value: 5 of 74 studies eligible; evidence quality very low to moderate
